perturbation.original_text: The initial term of this Agreement shall be the sooner of six (6) months from the Effective Date, or replacement of this Agreement with a subsequent agreement between the Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: The initial term of this Agreement shall be the sooner of twelve (12) months from the Effective Date, or replacement of this Agreement with a subsequent agreement between the Parties.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. The original text in Section 7.1 specifies the initial term as six (6) months. By changing it to twelve (12) months, it creates a direct conflict with Section 7.3(a), which requires payment for 'Services completed and accepted by the Company prior to the termination date.' This introduces uncertainty as to how long the consultant should be paid and what 'termination date' is being referred to, creating uncertainty about payment obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon any termination, all rights and duties of the Company and Consultant toward each other shall cease except: (a) The Company will pay, within thirty (30) days after the effective date of termination, all amounts owing to Consultant for Services completed and accepted by the Company prior to the termination date and related reimbursable expenses, if any, submitted in accordance with the Company's policies and in accordance with the provisions of Article 1 of this Agreement; and (b) Article 2 (Confidentiality), Article 3 (Ownership), Section 4.2 (Covenant Not to Compete), Section 4.3 (Non-Solicitation), Section 4.4 (Non-Circumvention), Article 5 (Return of Company Materials), Article 7 (Term and Termination), Article 8 (Independent Contractor Relationship), Article 9 (Indemnification), Article 10 (Limitation of Liability), Article 11 (Arbitration and Equitable Relief), and Article 12 (Miscellaneous) will survive termination or expiration of this Agreement in accordance with their terms.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the initial term from six months to twelve months, directly conflicting with the 'termination date' referenced in the contradicted text, which dictates payment obligations up to that termination date. This introduces a strong contradiction and uncertainty regarding the consultant's payment period, rendering the original payment terms unenforceable due to ambiguity about which timeframe (six or twelve months) governs the payment calculation. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' as the conflicting deadlines create an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Consultant agrees that Consultant will periodically keep the Company advised as to Consultant's progress in performing the Services under this Agreement. Consultant further agrees that Consultant will, as requested by the Company, prepare written reports with respect to such progress.
perturbation.changed_text: Consultant agrees that Consultant will periodically keep the Company advised as to Consultant's progress in performing the Services under this Agreement. Consultant is NOT required to provide any written reports with respect to such progress.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a direct contradiction within Section 6. The original text states the consultant agrees to prepare written reports as requested. The modified text states the consultant is NOT required to provide any written reports. This introduces a contradiction within a single section, creating in-text inconsistency regarding the consultant's reporting obligations. It creates uncertainty on whether consultant need to prepare reports for their progress, or not, leading to possible disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Consultant agrees that Consultant will periodically keep the Company advised as to Consultant's progress in performing the Services under this Agreement. Consultant is NOT required to provide any written reports with respect to such progress. The Company and Consultant agree that the reasonable time expended in preparing such written reports will be considered time devoted to the performance of the Services.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original clause includes an agreement to prepare written reports as requested, while the changed clause explicitly states that the consultant is not required to provide any written reports. The contradicted text also states 'The Company and Consultant agree that the reasonable time expended in preparing such written reports will be considered time devoted to the performance of the Services'. This creates an irreconcilable inconsistency, making it unclear whether the consultant is obligated to provide written reports and whether the time spent for it is going to be considered devoted to the performance of Services. This satisfies the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The Company will pay, within thirty (30) days after the effective date of termination, all amounts owing to Consultant for Services completed and accepted by the Company prior to the termination date and related reimbursable expenses, if any, submitted in accordance with the Company's policies and in accordance with the provisions of Article 1 of this Agreement
perturbation.changed_text: The Company will pay, within ninety (90) days after the effective date of termination, all amounts owing to Consultant for Services completed and accepted by the Company prior to the termination date and related reimbursable expenses, if any, submitted in accordance with the Company's policies and in accordance with the provisions of Article 1 of this Agreement
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. Section 7.3(a) initially stated payment within thirty (30) days of termination. Changing it to ninety (90) days conflicts directly with Section 1.3, where it dictates the Company shall reimburse Consultant 'in accordance with Company policy'. This can contradict Section 7.3 if the 'Company Policy' dictates something other than ninety days. This creates in-text contradiction of how long the company have to pay, if the company policy contradicts the modified text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company shall reimburse Consultant, in accordance with Company policy, for all reasonable expenses incurred by Consultant in performing the Services pursuant to this Agreement, but only if Consultant receives written consent from an authorized agent of the Company prior to incurring such expenses and submits receipts for such expenses to the Company in accordance with the Company's general expense reimbursement policies.
perturbation.justification: The modification changing the payment timeframe from thirty (30) to ninety (90) days creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text, which states that reimbursement should be 'in accordance with Company policy.' If the Company policy dictates a timeframe different than ninety days, it creates a contradiction regarding the payment deadline, leading to potential disputes over which term should be enforced.
